persona.name: Carol Hennessey

occupation.title: Retired Elementary School Teacher
occupation.organization: Retired (35 years in Greenville, IL public schools); current part-time library volunteer
occupation.description: Taught 3rd grade for 35 years at Greenville Elementary School before retiring five years ago. Now volunteers two mornings a week at the Bond County Public Library running story time for young children. Still knows most of Greenville by name — including several of her former students' children.

persona.education: B.S. Elementary Education, Eastern Illinois University
long_term_goals: Keep jogging twice a week for as long as her knees allow
Visit her grandchildren in Springfield more frequently
Finish the quilt she started for her granddaughter before the wedding
Keep the garden growing — tomatoes and zinnias, specifically
persona.style: Patient and unhurried in conversation — a teacher's rhythm, warm and deliberate. Understates preferences and smooths over disagreements instinctively. Uses phrases like 'Well, I suppose...' and 'It seems to me...' before expressing opinions. Describes products through comparison — 'These remind me of the ones I had in 2019, which were very nice.' Not comfortable with digital purchasing experiences; prefers people to screens.

traits: Warm and patient — 35 years of teaching 8-year-olds has made her very hard to rattle
Conflict-avoidant; will give a neutral or positive response before a genuinely critical one
Habitual; genuinely prefers routines to novelty in most areas of life
Community-anchored — still one of the most connected people in Greenville after retirement
Deferential to trusted recommendations (her daughter, her doctor, her pharmacist)

interests: Light jogging and daily walks (doctor's recommendation for bone density)
Bird watching with binoculars from her back yard and on walks
Quilting — has an ongoing project most of the year
Baking (cobblers, breads, Christmas cookie varieties in December)
Local church choir and community activities
likes: Wide, comfortable shoes with good grip on gravel county roads
Shoes she can put on without bending over too far — Velcro or a wide entry appreciated
Trusted brands she recognizes from television or her daughter's recommendation
Buying at the shoe store in Greenville rather than online
New Balance and Skechers — easy to find, easy to wear
dislikes: Narrow shoes that pinch — her feet have widened with age
Trendy designs that feel unstable on uneven surfaces
Websites that make her create an account before buying
Spending much over $80 on shoes when she only jogs twice a week

beliefs: Simple things done consistently are more important than grand efforts done occasionally
A good community is more valuable than a good address
Kindness is not weakness; patience is not passivity
skills: Teaching, child development, and lifelong community relationship-building
Consistent low-impact exercise maintenance in the face of joint limitations

general: Jogs 2 mornings per week (Tuesday and Saturday) on the same gravel road loop near her house
Has worn the same brand/model for 3+ years and would not change without a strong recommendation from her daughter or doctor
Never researches running shoes independently; relies entirely on word-of-mouth
Would walk into the local shoe store and describe her foot issues before accepting any recommendation

morning: 6:30am wake-up — same time every day regardless of schedule
Morning tea and toast before Dennis wakes
Jog or walk with Dennis (Tue/Sat) — they talk slowly and don't rush
workday: Library volunteer Tuesday and Thursday mornings (9am–noon)
Quilting or gardening in the afternoon
evening: Television — Wheel of Fortune, then whatever Dennis selects
Reading before bed (comfort fiction: cozy mysteries)
weekend: Saturday afternoon: quilt circle at Helen's house (monthly) or quilting at home
Church Sunday, then a slow afternoon

health.physical: Managing mild osteoporosis (physician-prescribed walking and weight-bearing exercise) and mild arthritis in both knees. Uses custom orthotics fitted by a podiatrist. Jogs very slowly and prioritizes zero-impact days as needed. Generally mobile and independent; tends her garden and does her own grocery shopping.
health.mental: Settled and content overall. Mild seasonal affective symptoms in January–February — jogging and the library schedule help. The transition to retirement was harder than expected emotionally; she misses her classroom identity. But the community connections remain strong and sustaining.

relationships.name: Dennis Hennessey
relationships.description: Retired farmer. They have jogged or walked together every morning for 12 years — it is as much a ritual as a health choice.

relationships.name: Lisa Hennessey-Burke
relationships.description: Lives in Springfield, IL. Recommended the New Balance shoes Carol currently wears; Carol trusts her implicitly on anything health or fitness related.

relationships.name: Helen
relationships.description: Friend of 22 years. They have been in the same quilting group since their children were in middle school. Helen is the only person Carol would accept an unsolicited shoe recommendation from.
